example: I'm beat. Time to hit the sack.
I'm still on the fence about moving abroad.
example_ko: 해외 이주에 대해 아직도 결정을 못 하겠어.
note: 울타리 위에 앉아 어느 쪽으로도 안 내려온 이미지

example: Don't cut corners on safety checks.
We've done enough. Let's call it a day.
I'm feeling a bit under the weather today.
note: 심각한 병이 아닌 가벼운 컨디션 난조

example: He told a joke to break the ice.
example_ko: 그가 어색한 분위기를 깨려고 농담을 했어.

expression: cost an arm and a leg
example: That new phone costs an arm and a leg.
note: 팔과 다리를 내줄 만큼 비싸다는 과장

example: He got cold feet right before the interview.
note: 결혼식 직전 장면에서 특히 자주 나옴

example: We hit it off on the first day.
I can't meet today. I'm swamped with work.
It's a big decision. Sleep on it.
example_ko: 중요한 결정이잖아. 하룻밤 자면서 생각해 봐.

example: Don't worry, we're all in the same boat.
example_ko: 걱정 마, 우리 다 같은 처지야.

example: No plans yet. Let's play it by ear.
example_ko: 아직 계획 없어. 상황 봐서 하자.
note: 악보 없이 귀로 연주한다는 데서 유래

example: That name rings a bell, but I can't place it.
example_ko: 그 이름 들어본 것 같은데 기억이 안 나.

example: I'm fed up with his excuses.
note: sick of, tired of보다 강한 느낌

example: She always goes the extra mile for her clients.
example_ko: 그녀는 고객을 위해 항상 한 발 더 뛰어.
note: OPIc에서 자기 강점 말할 때 유용

expression: off the top of my head
example: Off the top of my head, I'd say around fifty.
Stop beating around the bush and tell me.
If you keep working like this, you'll burn out.
example_ko: 이렇게 계속 일하면 번아웃 올 거야.

example: Can you keep an eye on my bag for a second?
She's a people person, perfect for sales.
I pulled an all-nighter to finish the project.
note: stay up all night의 명사형 관용구

example: On second thought, let's stay home tonight.
example_ko: 다시 생각해 보니 오늘은 집에 있자.

expression: give someone the benefit of the doubt
example: He's new, so let's give him the benefit of the doubt.
She was over the moon about the promotion.
example_ko: 걔 승진해서 완전 날아갈 듯 기뻐했어.

example: Speak of the devil! We were just talking about you.
example_ko: 호랑이도 제 말 하면 온다더니! 방금 네 얘기 하고 있었어.

example: You'll get the hang of it in a week.
Our travel plans are still up in the air.
note: 공중에 떠 있어 결정 안 된 상태

example: It's hard to make ends meet in this city.
Don't throw in the towel yet. We're so close.
example_ko: 아직 포기하지 마. 거의 다 왔어.

example: I only see him once in a blue moon.
example_ko: 걔는 정말 어쩌다 한 번 봐.

example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise.
example_ko: 그 일을 그만둔 게 오히려 전화위복이었어.

expression: bite off more than you can chew
example: I bit off more than I could chew with this project.
example_ko: 이 프로젝트는 내가 감당 못 할 일을 벌였어.
note: 씹을 수 있는 것보다 많이 물었다는 이미지

example: Keep me posted on the interview results.
Her cooking is second to none.
This noise is driving me crazy!
Exercising pays off in the long run.
The party got out of hand last night.
I'm torn between the two job offers.
example_ko: 두 회사 제안 사이에서 고민 중이야.

example: Taking that offer was a no-brainer.
example_ko: 그 제안을 받아들이는 건 고민할 것도 없었어.

example: Stop putting off the dentist appointment.
This app comes in handy when traveling.
She's famous but really down to earth.
I lost track of time playing games.
Great food, and the view was the icing on the cake.
I jog every morning, rain or shine.
example_ko: 난 비가 오든 해가 뜨든 매일 아침 조깅해.

example: Driving is second nature to me now.
I spent the morning running errands.
Let's make sure we're on the same page.
Don't jump to conclusions before hearing him out.
example_ko: 걔 얘기 다 듣기 전에 성급하게 결론 내리지 마.

expression: have a lot on my plate
example: I can't take on more work. I have a lot on my plate.
example_ko: 일 더 못 맡아. 지금도 할 일이 산더미야.

example: The little kid totally stole the show.
I don't plan much. I just go with the flow.
example_ko: 난 계획 별로 안 세워. 그냥 흐름에 맡겨.

example: After the marathon, I was completely worn out.
example_ko: 마라톤 뛰고 나서 완전 녹초가 됐어.

example: Her lecture gave me a lot of food for thought.
example_ko: 그녀의 강연은 생각할 거리를 많이 줬어.

example: He called me out of the blue after five years.

expression: take something with a grain of salt
example: Take online reviews with a grain of salt.
I'm hooked on this new series.
example_ko: 나 이 새 드라마에 완전 빠졌어.

example: Can you give me a hand with these boxes?
example_ko: 이 상자들 옮기는 것 좀 도와줄래?
note: 박수 치다(give a big hand)와 구분

example: In a nutshell, the project was a success.
It's getting late. Let's hit the road.
note: hit the gym(운동 가다)도 같은 패턴

example: The test was a piece of cake.
note: a walk in the park도 같은 뜻

example: I burned the midnight oil before the exam.
We don't always see eye to eye, but we respect each other.
example_ko: 우린 항상 의견이 같진 않지만 서로 존중해.

example: Her question caught me off guard.
This problem needs us to think outside the box.
After that mistake, he's on thin ice with the boss.
example_ko: 그 실수 이후로 걔는 상사한테 찍혀서 위태위태해.

expression: let the cat out of the bag
example: Who let the cat out of the bag about the party?
Sorry, I got carried away with the shopping.
Remote work is a win-win for everyone.
Read between the lines. She's not really okay.
example_ko: 행간을 읽어봐. 걔 사실 안 괜찮아.

example: Are you up for a hike this weekend?
example_ko: 이번 주말에 등산 갈 생각 있어?

example: I missed the boat on those concert tickets.
example_ko: 그 콘서트 티켓 살 기회를 놓쳤어.

example: Pull yourself together. We have work to do.
example_ko: 정신 차려. 우리 할 일이 있잖아.

expression: the ball is in your court
meaning: 이제 네 차례다, 네 결정에 달렸다
example: I made my offer. The ball is in your court.
example_ko: 내 제안은 했어. 이제 네가 결정할 차례야.

expression: know something like the back of my hand
example: I know this neighborhood like the back of my hand.
example_ko: 이 동네는 내 손바닥 보듯 훤해.

example: It's a long shot, but let's apply anyway.
I go boxing to blow off steam.

expression: cross that bridge when we come to it
example: Don't worry about next year. We'll cross that bridge when we come to it.
example_ko: 내년 일은 걱정 마. 닥치면 생각하자.

example: I have mixed feelings about moving out.
That trick worked like a charm.
example_ko: 그 방법 완전 기가 막히게 통했어.

example: The kids were glued to the screen all day.
example_ko: 애들이 하루 종일 화면에서 눈을 못 뗐어.

example: They're finally tying the knot next month!
A nice dinner doesn't have to break the bank.
example_ko: 근사한 저녁이라고 꼭 큰돈 들 필요는 없어.
note: 주로 부정문: won't break the bank(부담 없다)

example: He was caught red-handed eating my cake.
example_ko: 걔 내 케이크 먹다가 딱 걸렸어.
note: 손에 피가 묻은 채 잡힌 데서 유래

example: As a rule of thumb, save 20% of your income.
The plan failed, so we're back to square one.
Just giving you a heads-up: the boss is in a bad mood.
example_ko: 미리 말해두는데, 상사 기분 안 좋아.

example: Horror movies are not my cup of tea.
He's in hot water for missing the deadline.
example_ko: 걔 마감 못 지켜서 큰일 났어.

expression: kill two birds with one stone
example: Biking to work kills two birds with one stone.
Weekly check-ins help us stay on track.
example_ko: 주간 점검 덕분에 계획대로 갈 수 있어.

example: This new app is a total game changer.
example_ko: 이 새 앱은 완전 게임 체인저야.

example: Let's wrap up the meeting in ten minutes.

expression: be too good to be true
example: That deal sounds too good to be true.
example_ko: 그 조건은 너무 좋아서 오히려 수상해.

example: Look on the bright side: at least you tried.

expression: get a taste of your own medicine
example: He finally got a taste of his own medicine.
Tell me everything. I'm all ears.
You finally started exercising? Better late than never!
example_ko: 드디어 운동 시작했어? 늦게라도 하는 게 낫지!
note: 격려와 살짝 놀림 둘 다 가능

example: In this house, mom calls the shots.
